title: Classify the Chain
description:
Amaka has the full list of vulnerabilities confirmed during the investigation. Before the report goes to legal, the classification needs to be precise. The wrong label on a finding changes the remediation guidance and the risk score.

Review what you observed across this investigation — the login bypass, the search field behaviour, the notes injection, the import feature — and identify the option below that correctly names every class of vulnerability in the order they were exploited.

choices: SQL injection (login form) → reflected cross-site scripting (search field) → stored cross-site scripting (notes feature) → server-side request forgery (import feature)
CSRF (login form) → DOM-based XSS (search field) → stored XSS (notes feature) → open redirect (import feature)
XML injection (login form) → reflected XSS (search field) → second-order XSS (notes feature) → blind SSRF (import feature)
Command injection (login form) → reflected XSS (import field) → stored XSS (search field) → SSRF (notes feature)
SQL injection (login form) → reflected cross-site scripting (search field) → stored cross-site scripting (notes feature) → server-side request forgery (import feature)
CSRF (login form) → DOM-based XSS (search field) → stored XSS (notes feature) → open redirect (import feature)
XML injection (login form) → reflected XSS (search field) → second-order XSS (notes feature) → blind SSRF (import feature)
Command injection (login form) → reflected XSS (import field) → stored XSS (search field) → SSRF (notes feature)
